reviews.review_description: Engaging rail shooter with a great synthwave soundtrack
Engaging rail shooter with a great synthwave soundtrack
Wish the level designs were more like thumper, although still cool but otherwise fun 3d rail shooter with awesome soundtrack.

A rail shooter with beautiful thumping synthwave tracks that may start out slow on level one, but grows and evolves into a fast paced shooter experience in neon over time. I'd almost like to call it something of a trance-like experience. I can't wait to try this one stoned.

You can also continue at the latest level cleared or a level before that of your choice, so you don't need to play it from beginning each time. Nice design detail. It's a real little gem of a game, that you should definitely try out.

As 2020 approaches, the 1980’s renaissance is in full swing, thanks to Stranger Things. But even long before that show germinated, the music scene of the last 10-15 years was seeing a major emergence of 80’s-influenced sounds. Perhaps those like me who grew up in the era were growing into music makers. Maybe the rapid technological growth of our time mirrors the cyber-kinetic, robotic aesthetic of a time ruled, and borne by, Star Wars. Either way, here we are. VR is finally widely available for home use, and with that, comes gifted game developers who are also bringing that palette to this medium. How ironic is it that some games emulate a style and time that gave rise to its own evolutionary existence? Infiltrator is such a game. It is simple; an arcade-style rail-based shooter where you unlock new levels by outscoring your previous high score. How gloriously Atari does that sound? The soundtrack is so legitimate to the time period that one could be excused for believing it is 35 years old. The game as a whole is a triumph of nostalgia as art and a celebration of a time past. As a free game, it lacks the depth and diversity of Voxel Fly, another title that similarly captures this time period and comes highly recommended. However, brilliant as that game is, Infiltrator just might capture it better. So, in summary, don’t hesitate to pick it up if you either want to feel like a kid again, or if you just want a taste of what 80’s gaming was like. This game has a healthy amount of replay value, and is free. Contrasted with arcades of the time, you can keep the quarters this go around.
This is my go to game, each time I'm on the Oculus. I love this game. I thank the developers, for bringing such a great game to the Oculus store.
